content.problemStatement:
Chef has started a new job as an insurance agent.

Each insurance costs XXX dollars and Chef gets a 20%20 \%20% commission on selling each insurance.

Find the **minimum** number of insurances Chef needs to sell to earn **at least** 100100100 dollars.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer XXX, the cost of an insurance.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of insurances Chef needs to sell to earn **at least** 100100100 dollars.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the cost (in dollars) of one insurance policy.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a separate line the minimum number of insurances Chef must sell to earn at least 100 dollars commission.

solution.approach: For each test case, the commission earned per insurance is 20% of X, i.e., X/5 dollars. To reach at least 100 dollars we need n * (X/5) >= 100 => n >= 500 / X. The smallest integer n satisfying this is the ceiling of 500/X. Using integer arithmetic, n = (500 + X - 1) / X.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        x = int(data[idx]); idx += 1
        ans = (500 + x - 1) // x  # ceiling of 500/x
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
